step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard diagram and transcript, the user initiates a request from the self-service portal (EC2) which routes to EKS. EKS processes the request by saving metadata in PostgreSQL (RDS) and pushing a message to SQS. A background job on EKS retrieves the SQS message to provision target infrastructure (EMR, Glue Catalog, S3, EC2 for Airflow/NiFi, IAM/Ranger). Finally, EKS triggers SES to notify the user of completion.

graph.name: Self-Service Enterprise Data Engineering Platform
graph.notes: Automates compliant, secure infrastructure provisioning and data access control for life sciences data engineers and analysts, reducing wait times from weeks to minutes.

nodes.name: Data Engineers & Analysts
nodes.notes: Internal users requesting infrastructure or data access.

nodes.name: Self-Service Portal
nodes.notes: Web portal for selecting infrastructure types and cost center tracking.

nodes.name: EKS Core Platform
nodes.notes: Hosts 100+ microservices for computing lifecycle, provisioning, and workflow orchestration.

nodes.name: PostgreSQL
nodes.notes: Stores request records and configuration metadata.

nodes.name: SQS Queue
nodes.notes: Queues events for automated infrastructure and data access workflows.

nodes.name: SES
nodes.notes: Sends approval requests and status notifications back to users.

nodes.name: Glue Catalog
nodes.notes: Manages databases and controls access permissions.

nodes.name: S3 Data Lake
nodes.notes: Secure storage buckets created for data residency.

nodes.name: IAM & Ranger
nodes.notes: Applies role policies and fine-grained access controls.

edges.notes: Forward request details to EKS microservices.
EKS worker polls message to start setup.
Integrates S3 data access through Glue.
